LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT WITNESS CONVENIENCE AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WILL BE PROMOTED BY A TRANSFER OF THIS CASE TO ORANGE COUNTY. MR. FLAHERTY IS REQUIRED TO PAY ALL TRANSFER COSTS WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ALL NON-PARTY WITNESSES WOULD BE SIGNIFICANTLY INCONVENIENCED IF THEY WERE REQUIRED TO ATTEND TRIAL IN SAN FRANCISCO. ALTHOUGH SOME UBER WITNESSES MAY LIVE IN THE BAY AREA AND THUS TRIAL IN SAN FRANCISCO IS MORE CONVENIENT FOR THEM THAN TRIAL IN ORANGE COUNTY, THIS FACT DOES NOT OUTWEIGH THE INCONVENIENCE TO THE NON-PARTY WITNESSES. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-12-2017 Vacated

Notice Of Joinder In Defendant Robert Flahertys Motion For Oder Changing Venue
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jenny Anglin of The Simon Law Group telephone number 714-916-5050 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Kirk Skoog.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC and RASIER-CA, LLC'S Joinder In Defendant Robert Flahertys Motion For Oder Changing Venue is off calendar in light of the ruling on the motion sought to be joined.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jenny Anglin of The Simon Law Group telephone number 714-916-5050 appeared by telephone for Plaintiff Kirk Skoog.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Ms. Anglin stated that she has nothing to add. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT FLAHERTY'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE, to Orange County is granted. Defendant has met his burden of showing that witness convenience and the ends of justice will be promoted by a transfer of this case to Orange County. Mr. Flaherty is required to pay all transfer costs within five days of this order. All non-party witnesses would be significantly inconvenienced if they were required to attend trial in San Francisco. Although some Uber witnesses may live in the Bay Area and thus trial in San Francisco is more convenient for them than trial in Orange County, this fact does not outweigh the inconvenience to the non-party witnesses. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-16-2016 Set For Trial On Jun-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Joinder In Defendant Robert Flahertys Motion For Oder Changing Venue
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Brandon J. Simon of The Simon Law Group (855-855-8910) appears telephonically for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Adrian L. Canzoneri of Wilson Elser et al LLP (415-433-0990) appears for defendant, Uber Technologies

Attorney, Melis Atalay of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (415-362-6000) appears for defendant/moving party, Robert Flaherty

The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC and RASIER-CA, LLC's Joinder In Defendant Robert Flahertys Motion For Oder Changing Venue is Off calendar in light of the ruling on the underlying motion.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Changing Venue
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Attorney, Brandon J. Simon of The Simon Law Group (855-855-8910) appears telephonically for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Adrian L. Canzoneri of Wilson Elser et al LLP (415-433-0990) appears for defendant, Uber Technologies

Attorney, Melis Atalay of Hinshaw & Culbertson LLP (415-362-6000) appears for defendant/moving party, Robert Flaherty

The cause is argued and submitted by all sides as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ROBERT FLAHERTY's Motion For Order Changing Venue is denied without prejudice to re-filing the motion based on witness convenience after all defendants have filed answers. The motion to change venue on the grounds that plaintiff Kirk Skoog filed this case in the wrong court is denied. Even when all of the defendants join in a demand for or consent to a change of venue, a case is filed in a proper venue if the plaintiff attempts in apparent good faith to state a cause of action against a defendant who resides in the county where the case is commenced. (Monogram Co. of Cal. v. Kingsley (1951) 38 Cal.2d 28.) Both Uber Technologies, Inc. and Raiser-CA, LLC are residents of San Francisco and thus San Francisco is a proper venue. A motion to transfer venue based on witness convenience cannot be made before an answer is filed. (Buran Equip. Co. v. Superior Court (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662.) No answers have been filed. The case law cited by plaintiff does not conclusively bar a defendant from re-filing a motion to change venue on the basis of witness convenience once an answer has been filed. (McNiel & Co. v. Doe (1912) 163 Cal.338; Connell v. Bowes (1942) 49 Cal.App.2d 542.) If such a motion is filed later in the case, the moving party must submit declarations that show the names of each witness expected to testify for all parties, the substance of their expected testimony, whether the witness has been deposed or has given a statement regarding the facts of the case, the reasons why it would be inconvenient for the witnesses to appear locally, and the reason why the ends of justice would be promoted by transfer to a different county. (Weil and Brown book at 3:576.) The declarations must contain admissible evidence. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)
